title: Digest — Mar 24, 2026, 07:43 AM UTC
summary:
# Pwarf Devlog Digest

Hey Pwarf fans! We’ve had some exciting updates since our last digest. Here’s a quick rundown of what’s new in the game:

## Database Cleanup
- **Schema Updates**: We streamlined our database by dropping 7 unused tables, including `expeditions` and `factions`. We also removed some dead columns from the `dwarves`, `worlds`, and `players` tables, making our data more efficient.

## Cave System Enhancements
- **Scouting & Planning**: We reworked the cave system to feature per-entrance lazy generation, meaning that each entrance leads to its own unique cave grid.
- **User Experience**: You can now scout caves by clicking on entrances, which sends a `scout_cave` task to discover the cave upon completion.

## Combat & Interaction Improvements
- **Entity Stacking**: There’s no longer any stacking of entities; dwarves and monsters cannot occupy the same tile. Combat triggers now occur from adjacent tiles, enhancing tactical gameplay.
- **Clickable Monsters**: Monsters are now interactable on the map! Clicking on them opens a detailed view with stats and lore.

## General Cleanup
- **Removed Features**: The idle-wandering phase and the smooth/engrave designations have been removed. This decluttering helps us focus on more impactful gameplay aspects.

We’re excited to continue refining and improving Pwarf, so stay tuned for more updates!
